Contrast involves footsteps (one and two persons) vs REST; task probes social auditory information and mentalizing about agents. Global and local inclusion criteria satisfied.
Task does not measure affiliation or attachment constructs (no kinship, cooperation, or close-relationship manipulation); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Listening to human footsteps is an auditory social stimulus (receptive non-facial communication); the contrast isolates processing of social auditory cues. Global and local inclusion criteria satisfied.
Contrast does not involve self-related judgments or stimuli; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Contrast probes perception of other agents (biological motion and identity cues from footsteps); meets perception-of-others criteria.
This contrast uses matched noise control stimuli (NOISE1+NOISE2) which are not social stimuli; does not meet SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1 and SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Noise-control contrast does not measure affiliation or attachment; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Noise-control stimuli are non-social auditory bursts and do not measure social communication (receptive social auditory processing); missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Contrast is not self-related; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Noise-control contrast does not probe perception/understanding of others (no biological/social agents); missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
(STEP2 - NOISE2) - (STEP1 - NOISE1) targets differential processing of two-person vs one-person footstep scenes, isolating social-complexity effects; global and local inclusion criteria satisfied.
Contrast does not operationalize affiliation/attachment (no kinship/cooperation or close-relationship manipulation); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
This differential contrast isolates social auditory information (two vs one agents) and thus probes receptive non-facial social communication; global and local inclusion criteria satisfied.
Contrast concerns perception of others/group dynamics, not self-related processing; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Contrast does not assess affiliation/attachment constructs; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Isolation of auditory biological motion (footsteps) vs matched noise is a receptive non-facial social-communication task; global and local inclusion criteria satisfied.
Not a self-related task; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Contrast probes perception of another agent via footsteps (biological motion/identity cues), meeting perception-of-others criteria.
STEP2 - NOISE2 isolates processing of two-person footstep scenes, which the authors interpret as engaging socio-emotional and social-cognitive networks; global and local criteria satisfied.
Contrast does not manipulate affiliation/attachment or close relationships; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Two-person footstep auditory scenes are social auditory stimuli; the contrast measures reception of non-facial social communicative cues, satisfying local criteria.
Not assessing self-related processing; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Contrast probes perception and inference about other agents (group dynamics, identity cues), meeting perception-of-others inclusion criteria.

Contrast does not operationalize affiliation/attachment constructs (no cooperation/kinship/close-relationship contrasts); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Analysis contrasts social versus individual actions in healthy adults using whole-brain group-level fMRI, directly measuring social processing of observed interactions.
The task does not measure affiliation/attachment constructs (e.g., kinship, cooperation/competition, loved-one vs stranger). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Main effect of type of observed action contrasts social vs individual actions but does not specifically target social communication constructs (e.g., gaze/face perception). Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Task does not probe self-related processing (no self-judgment or viewing self). Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Contrasts probe perception and understanding of others (social vs individual actions; inferring others' goals/relations) in healthy adults using whole-brain group-level fMRI.
Analysis tests main effect of gaze (presence vs absence) on observation of actions in healthy adults with whole-brain group fMRI, thus measuring social processing related to gaze cues.
The gaze manipulation does not target affiliation/attachment constructs (e.g., kinship, cooperation). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Main effect of gaze directly manipulates availability of eye-gaze cues (masked vs available), a canonical social communication measure, in healthy adults with whole-brain fMRI.
No measures of self-related processing are present in this contrast. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Gaze availability affects neural responses involved in perceiving and interpreting others' actions and intentions (pSTS, amygdala), meeting perception-of-others criteria in healthy whole-brain fMRI.
Interaction tests how gaze modulates processing of social versus individual actions (dMPFC modulation), a social-processing effect measured with whole-brain group fMRI in healthy adults.
Interaction concerns gaze modulation of social inference, not affiliation/attachment per se. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Interaction explicitly involves gaze availability affecting interpretation of social interactions (eye-gaze is a core social-communication cue); fits social communication criteria in whole-brain healthy adult analysis.
Contrast investigates others' interactions and gaze, not self-related processes. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Interaction reflects inferential processing about others' social relations (dMPFC) and how gaze availability affects understanding others, meeting perception-of-others criteria in whole-brain healthy adult fMRI.

This analysis does not involve self-related judgments or viewing the self; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Stories>Rest is a whole-brain group contrast of a social reasoning task (Wason selection stories) that measures social processing broadly (interpretation of social-exchange, precautionary, and descriptive social rules).
Although the task includes social-exchange content, this contrast is collapsed across all story types and does not specifically isolate affiliation/attachment (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
The Wason reasoning stories do not measure social communication constructs such as face/gesture perception or gaze (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
The contrast does not involve self-related judgments or viewing the self; PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 is not met.

While the overall task involves reasoning about others, this collapsed Stories>Rest contrast does not specifically isolate Theory-of-Mind/understanding-others processes (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 not specifically isolated in this contrast).
Rest>Stories is an inverse contrast showing greater signal at rest than during stories; it does not directly measure social processing in the task condition (SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2 not met).
Contrast does not isolate affiliation/attachment processes (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Contrast does not measure face/gaze/communication processes (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
Contrast does not involve self-related processing (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).
Rest>Stories does not index perception/understanding of others; the contrast is not measuring ToM-like processes (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 not met).
Cards>Rest is a whole-brain group contrast of the decision/violation-detection phase of the social reasoning task and therefore indexes social processing broadly.
This collapsed card-phase contrast mixes social-exchange, precautionary, and descriptive cards and does not specifically isolate affiliation/attachment (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Card-choice events do not probe facial/communicative reception or production (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
Card decisions do not involve explicit self-related judgments (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).

Although cards require judgments about other people, the collapsed Cards>Rest contrast does not specifically isolate ToM/understanding-others processes (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 not specifically isolated).
Rest>Cards is an inverse contrast (rest greater than card responses) and does not directly index social processing in the task condition (SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2 not met).
Contrast does not isolate affiliation/attachment processes (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Contrast does not measure social communication constructs (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
Contrast does not involve self-related processing (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).
Rest>Cards does not index perception/understanding of others; the contrast is not measuring ToM-like processes (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 not met).

Stories: Social Contracts > Precautions is a whole-brain group contrast that isolates social-exchange (cheater-detection) processing versus precautionary processing; this directly measures social processing.
This contrast isolates social-exchange (cheater detection/cooperation) processing (affiliation/cooperation), matching AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and I2.
The contrast targets social-exchange reasoning rather than face/gaze/communicative perception or production; SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met.
Contrast does not probe self-reflection or self-judgment processes (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).
Interpreting social contracts requires inferring others' desires/intentions (ToM); the contrast isolates regions associated with understanding others (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and I2).
Stories: Social Contracts > Descriptives isolates social-exchange interpretation and therefore measures social processing.
This contrast isolates social-exchange (cooperation/cheater detection) relative to descriptive rules, matching AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/I2.
Contrast focuses on social-exchange reasoning rather than communicative signal processing (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
Contrast does not involve self-related judgments or self-viewing (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).
Social contract interpretation engages Theory-of-Mind/understanding-others processes; this contrast isolates those processes (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/I2).

Cards: Social Contracts > Precautions contrasts card-phase violation detection for social-exchange vs precautionary rules; it is a whole-brain group contrast of social decision events and thus measures social processing broadly.
The card phase contrast isolates detection of social-exchange violations (cheater detection), which indexes affiliation/cooperation processes (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/I2).
Card-choice contrasts do not probe communicative signal processing (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
Card decisions do not involve self-related judgments (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).

Although cards concern other people's behavior, the post-interpretive violation-detection phase did not differentially engage ToM in this study and thus this contrast does not specifically measure perception/understanding of others as ToM (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 not met for this phase).
Cards: Social Contracts > Descriptives contrasts card-phase responses for social-exchange vs descriptive rules and therefore measures social processing during decision making.
This contrast isolates social-exchange violation detection relative to descriptive rules, indexing affiliation/cooperation (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/I2).
Card-choice contrasts do not probe communicative reception/production processes (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
This contrast does not involve self-related judgments (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).

The decision/violation-detection card phase for social contracts did not differentially engage ToM regions in the reported results, so this contrast does not specifically index perception/understanding of others (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 not met for the card phase).
Stories: Precautions > Social Contracts isolates interpretation of precautionary social rules (hazard management) and thus indexes social processing broadly (SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1).
Precautionary rules are about hazard/risks, not affiliation/cooperation specifically (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Precaution interpretation does not probe face/gaze/communicative processes (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
Precaution stories do not involve self-judgment or viewing self (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).
Interpretation of precautions does not require inferring others' mental states (ToM) by design; PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 is not met for this contrast.
Stories: Precautions > Descriptives isolates precautionary rule interpretation (hazard management) and thus indexes social processing broadly.
Precautionary reasoning addresses hazards and risk management, not affiliation/cooperation specifically (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Precautionary story interpretation does not probe social communication such as faces or gaze (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
Precautionary stories do not engage self-judgment or self-viewing (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).
Precautionary rule interpretation is not intended to invoke Theory-of-Mind inferences about others' mental states; PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 not met.
Cards: Precautions > Social Contracts contrasts violation-detection for precautionary versus social-exchange rules and thus measures social-related decision processes.
This card-phase contrast isolates precautionary violation detection (risk/hazard) rather than affiliation/cooperation; AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met.
Card decision contrasts do not probe communicative signal processing (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
The contrast does not involve self-related judgments (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).

Although some posterior temporal activation was observed for precaution cards, the contrast does not specifically index ToM/understanding-others processes; PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 is not the primary target of this contrast.
Cards: Precautions > Descriptives contrasts card-phase violation detection for precautions vs descriptives and thus indexes social-related decision processes.
Precautionary violation detection is about hazard/risk, not affiliation/cooperation specifically (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Card-response contrasts do not probe face/gaze/communication processing (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
This contrast does not involve self-referential judgments (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).
Precaution vs descriptive card detection does not primarily measure ToM/understanding-others processes (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 not met).

Stories: Descriptives > Social Contracts contrasts interpretation of descriptive social rules about people versus social-exchange and thus involves social processing (judgments about others' habits/preferences).
Descriptive social rules concern traits/preferences rather than affiliation/cooperation per se (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Descriptive story contrasts do not probe face/gaze/communicative processing (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
Descriptive stories involve judgments about others, not the self (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).

Although descriptive stories are about others, this specific Descriptives>SocialContracts contrast produced no significant clusters and does not specifically isolate ToM/understanding-others processes here (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 not clearly met).
Stories: Descriptives > Precautions contrasts interpretation of descriptive social rules about people versus precaution rules and thus involves social processing (affective/social trait inference).
Descriptive stories concern traits/preferences rather than affiliation/cooperation specifically (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Descriptive>Precaution story contrast does not target face/gaze/communication processing (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
This contrast does not involve self-judgment or viewing the self (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).

Descriptive stories activated right parahippocampal/amygdala regions associated with social/affective processing of others in the reported results, so this contrast indexes perception/understanding of others (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/I2).
Cards: Descriptives > Social Contracts contrasts card-phase responses for descriptive versus social-exchange rules and thus indexes social-related decision processing.
Card-phase descriptive responses do not target affiliation/cooperation per se (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Card-choice contrasts do not probe communicative/perceptual face or gaze processes (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
This contrast does not involve self-referential judgments (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).

Although descriptive card decisions concern others, this card-phase contrast primarily engaged deliberative reasoning regions rather than ToM-specific regions; PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 is not met for this phase.
Cards: Descriptives > Precautions contrasts card-phase responses for descriptive versus precautionary rules and thus indexes social-related decision processing broadly.
Card-phase descriptives do not isolate affiliation/cooperation processes (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 not met).
Card-choice contrasts do not target communicative signal processing such as faces/gaze (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 not met).
This contrast does not involve self-related processing (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 not met).

Group-level whole-brain contrasts compare self and friend recognition (face and name), a social-related task measuring social processing of person identity; meets global and local criteria.
Contrast tests self vs friend, not affiliation/attachment (Loved one vs Stranger); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast involves recognition of faces and names (receptive social signals) in a group-level whole-brain task; meets global and social-communication criteria.
This analysis directly contrasts self vs friend (Self > Other), measuring perception/understanding of self; meets global and perception_self criteria.
Contrast is self > friend so does not measure perception/understanding of others (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2).
Face simple-effect (Sf–Ff) is a group-level whole-brain contrast of social recognition (self vs friend faces), meeting global and social processing criteria.
Contrast compares self face vs friend face, not loved-one vs stranger; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
This is a face viewing contrast (self face vs friend face) which fits reception of facial communication in a whole-brain task.
Direct self > friend face contrast (Self > Other) measures perception/understanding of self (face-based).
Contrast is self face > friend face and does not index perception/understanding of others (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2).
Name simple-effect (Sn–Fn) is a group-level whole-brain contrast of social recognition using names (self vs friend), meeting global and social processing criteria.

This is a name-based social recognition contrast (receptive non-facial social signal) in a whole-brain task, satisfying social-communication criteria.
Direct self > friend name contrast (Self > Other) measures perception/understanding of self (name-based).
Contrast is self name > friend name and does not index perception/understanding of others (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2).

Analyzes face-specific self-recognition (viewing faces) and therefore fits reception of facial communication in a whole-brain contrast.

Focus is on self-face specificity, not on perception/understanding of others (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2).

This analysis is a group-level, whole-brain, voxelwise exploratory analysis that links social interaction behavior (cooperation difference in in-group vs out-group PD game) to BOLD differences — it measures social processing related to interactive social behavior.

The analysis explicitly examines cooperation (affiliation-related behavior) differences between in-group and out-group partners and correlates that behavioral difference with whole-brain BOLD differences, matching affiliation/attachment inclusion criteria.

Although the analysis concerns social interaction, it does not target social communication constructs (e.g., facial/expressive communication, gaze, face perception) as required by SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/I2.

The analysis focuses on between-partner (in-group vs out-group) cooperative behavior and neural correlates of those differences, not on self-judgment or self > other contrasts; it does not meet PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/I2.

The analysis probes neural correlates of understanding others (theory-of-mind/other-directed social inference) by linking behavioral discrimination (cooperation difference) with BOLD differences during in-group vs out-group interactions, satisfying perception-of-others criteria.

Global criteria met (healthy adult sample, group-level whole-brain task-evoked analysis). The task is an explicit social interaction simulation (friends/peers vs celebrities, approach/avoid), and the contrasts measure social processing related to affiliation and emotional valence.

Global criteria met. The analysis directly contrasts interactions with 'friends' (positive peers) versus other peers/celebrities, which operationalizes affiliation/attachment; results and interaction effects specifically identify friend-related activations.

Global criteria met. The task involves viewing faces (front-facing photographs with direct gaze) and responding to social-emotional cues (approach/avoid), which fits Social Communication (reception of facial/affective cues).

Global criteria met but the analysis does not measure self-related processing (no self-judgment, no viewing of the participant's own picture, and contrasts are not Self > Other). Missing required local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global criteria met. The task probes perception/understanding of others (empathy, recognition of others' emotional/relational significance) and interaction contrasts (friends vs others) directly map onto Perception of Others constructs.

Healthy adult sample and whole-brain group fMRI random-effects analyses meet global criteria. The Communication vs Control contrast measures communicative social behavior and thus assesses social processing.
The Communication vs Control contrast does not test affiliation/attachment (no loved-one vs stranger or related affiliation contrast); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global criteria met. The Communication vs Control contrast measures production of communicative speech (a social-communication production task).
No tasks or contrasts involve self-judgment or self-referential processing; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met. The Communication vs Control contrast involves understanding and interacting with an actor (perception/understanding of others, including mental-state/context processing).
Healthy adult whole-brain group analysis; Description vs Control involves observing and describing social actions and situations, and thus assesses social processing.
The Description vs Control contrast does not manipulate affiliation/familiarity (no loved-one vs stranger contrast); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

The Description task involves describing the actor's situation rather than producing communicative speech directed to the actor; it does not meet SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2 (contrast does not index social communication production/reception).
No self-referential judgments or self-related processing in the Description vs Control contrast; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met. The Description vs Control contrast involves perception and understanding of another person's actions and situation (action perception/theory of mind relevant).
Global criteria met. The Communication vs Description contrast isolates behavioral aspects of communicative speech (a social task) and thus measures social processing.
This contrast isolates communication-specific processes but does not manipulate affiliation/familiarity (no loved-one vs stranger contrast); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global criteria met. Communication vs Description specifically indexes communicative speech production (production of social communication), satisfying SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and I2.
No self-related tasks or contrasts are present in Communication vs Description; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met. The Communication vs Description contrast involves understanding others' intentions/biographical information and social context (theory-of-mind/perception of others).
Global criteria met. The Familiar vs Unfamiliar Communication contrast directly probes social processing related to familiarity/biographical information in communicative contexts.
Global criteria met. The Familiar vs Unfamiliar Communication contrast matches affiliation/attachment-type contrasts (loved one/known person vs stranger), indexing affiliation-related processing.
Global criteria met. The contrast examines communication directed to familiar vs unfamiliar actors — a manipulation within social communication (production) context.
No self-referential or self-judgment processes are manipulated in the Familiar vs Unfamiliar Communication contrast; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met. This contrast probes differences in perception/understanding of others (familiar vs unfamiliar), including access to biographical/mental-state information.

Group-level, whole-brain task contrasts in a social interactive competitive aggression paradigm measure social processing (perception, interpretation and response to social cues).

Although the task is social and competitive, this contrast (receiving aversive stimuli / anticipation of punishment) does not measure affiliation/attachment (positive bonding or relationship formation). Missing required local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
The contrast reflects anticipation of punishment and not the reception or production of communicative signals (faces/expressions) per se — missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
This condition indexes anticipation of aversive stimulation in a social context rather than explicit self-judgment, self-referential evaluation, or agency tasks — missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
The contrast centers on anticipating being punished (self-focused) rather than explicit perception/understanding of others' mental states or empathy — missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Group-level, whole-brain parametric modulation of retaliation intensity during an interactive social task indexes social processing (decision-making in social interaction).
Retaliation/rewtribution is part of competitive behavior but the contrast measures aggressive decision-making rather than affiliation/attachment or bonding — missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

The retaliation parametric modulation indexes decision processes and motor/visual components, not explicit reception or production of communicative signals (faces/expressions) required for SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Although the task involves making a choice, it does not operationalize explicit self-judgment, self-report, or self-reference contrasts required for PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Retaliation involves representing the opponent and anticipating their suffering; the parametric modulation relates to social-cognitive processes about others (mental states/empathy), satisfying perception-of-others criteria.
Conjunction of retaliation and watching the opponent shows common activation in canonical social brain regions (mPFC, STS, amygdala), indicating broad social processing.
The conjunction reflects social interaction and empathy/mentalizing in a competitive/retaliatory context rather than measures of affiliation/attachment or bonding — missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
The conjunction includes activation in STS, fusiform and other regions involved in processing others' facial/body expressions (video of opponent suffering), meeting SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and I2.
The conjunction emphasizes perception/processing of the opponent and decision-making rather than self-referential judgments or explicit self vs other contrasts — missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Conjunction highlights regions (STS, amygdala, mPFC) whose activity correlates with perceived pain/emotional state of the opponent, satisfying PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and I2.

Contrast SOC>ARB tests perception of socially relevant facial expressions vs arbitrary movements; sample and whole-brain group-level map meet global criteria.
Does not measure affiliation/attachment (no cooperation/kinship or loved-one vs stranger contrasts). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
SOC>ARB probes reception of facial communication (socially relevant expressions) in a whole-brain group contrast; global criteria met.
Contrast does not measure self-related processing (no Self>Other contrast). Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
SOC>ARB involves perception and decoding of others' socially relevant facial expressions (fits Perception of Others); global criteria met.
ARB>SOC isolates processing of arbitrary (non-social) facial movements rather than social processing. Missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Does not measure affiliation/attachment (no cooperation/kinship or loved-one vs stranger contrasts). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast emphasizes arbitrary/non-communicative facial movements (non-social); does not meet SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Does not measure self-related processing (no Self>Other contrast). Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Contrasts arbitrary movements > social movements and does not specifically probe understanding of others' mental states or social intent. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
ME>OTHER contrasts self-directed gaze (personal involvement) vs other-directed, indexing social involvement; global criteria met.
Task does not target affiliation/attachment constructs (no measures of bonding, cooperation, or loved-one vs stranger). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
ME>OTHER contrasts direct gaze (an ostensive communicative signal) vs averted gaze; fits Social Communication reception of facial/gaze cues and is a whole-brain group contrast.
ME>OTHER is a canonical Self>Other contrast (self-involvement vs other), directly measuring Perception/Understanding of Self.
ME>OTHER contrasts self-directed vs other-directed attention and does not primarily probe Perception/Understanding of Others (Other>Self would). Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
OTHER>ME indexes processing when social signals are directed to another (third-person perspective) and thus probes social processing; global criteria met.
Task does not measure affiliation/attachment constructs (no bonding/cooperation contrasts). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
OTHER>ME concerns perception of gaze/expressions directed to others (reception of social signals) and meets Social Communication criteria in a whole-brain contrast.
OTHER>ME is Other>Self, not a Self>Other contrast; does not meet PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
OTHER>ME explicitly probes perception/understanding of others (third-person perspective) and aligns with Perception of Others criteria; global criteria met.
The SOC×ME interaction isolates effects of social signals when the participant is personally involved (second-person phenomena) and therefore indexes social processing; global criteria met.
Interaction does not directly measure affiliation/attachment constructs (no bonding/cooperation measures). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
SOC×ME indexes how communicative facial expressions are processed when directed at the self (reception of facial/gaze communication); meets Social Communication criteria.
SOC×ME specifically tests social signals when the self is the addressee (self-relevance); aligns with Perception/Self criteria.
This interaction isolates self-directed social effects rather than perception of others per se. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
SOC×OTHER probes processing of social signals when directed to another (third-person social perception) and therefore qualifies as social processing; global criteria met.
Interaction does not target affiliation/attachment constructs (no bonding/cooperation contrasts). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
SOC×OTHER addresses how social communicative signals are processed when directed to others (reception of facial/gaze cues about others); meets Social Communication criteria.
SOC×OTHER emphasizes other-directed processing rather than self-related processing. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
SOC×OTHER explicitly probes perception/understanding of others (social signals directed to another), aligning with Perception of Others criteria; global criteria met.

The analysis is a group-level whole-brain fMRI contrast on healthy adults using a social-emotion judgment task (guilt and embarrassment) that the authors link explicitly to social cognition/Theory of Mind, satisfying both global and local social-processing criteria.

The task contrasts guilt and embarrassment and does not measure affiliation/attachment constructs (no kinship, cooperation, close-relationship, or loved-one vs stranger comparisons). Missing required inclusion criteria.

The contrasts probe evaluative processes of self-conscious moral emotions that the authors interpret as requiring representation of others' mental states (Theory of Mind); the analysis is whole-brain, group-level, and maps onto perception/understanding of others.
